target: data/sections.json part1 SEC-1.1 — B(r) formula version attribution
finding: The dome's two-pole B(r) formula with coefficients A=62,376 and C=64,852 (both using λ=8,619 km) appears as WIN-053, confirmed 2026-03-23 in V51.1. However, the dome's own model page (model.html) lists the two-pole framework as OPEN-011 with "coefficients A and C undetermined" and "scale lengths λ_1 and λ_2 unknown pending fitting." This means the wins page claims WIN-053 as CONFIRMED while the model page treats the same formula as an open question with undetermined parameters. This is an additional self-contradiction worth noting.

patches.location: data/sections.json → part1 HTML, near "Two-pole geomagnetic field B(r)"
patches.replacement_text: Two-pole geomagnetic field B(r) = 62,376×e<sup>−r_N/8,619</sup> + 64,852×e<sup>−r_S/8,619</sup> nT (WIN-053, added at V51.0; notably, the dome's own model page still lists this as OPEN-011 with "undetermined" coefficients — confirmed on the wins page, unresolved on the model page).
patches.reason: ISS-869: formula presented without noting when introduced or its contradictory status across dome pages. The contradiction between WIN-053 (confirmed) and OPEN-011 (undetermined) strengthens our argument.

kernel_of_truth: The two-pole extension is a reasonable approach — adding a southern magnetic source to a single-pole model does reduce global RMS error. The dome reports going from 61% to 20% RMS error, which is expected from adding a second exponential term regardless of the physics.

anticipated_objections.objection: The model page may not have been updated yet to reflect WIN-053 confirmation.
anticipated_objections.response: Even if so, this highlights a general pattern: the wins page runs ahead of the technical documentation. Claims are marked "confirmed" before the underlying model page is updated to match. This is documented in our Pattern 4 (announcement precedes documentation) analysis.

note_on_ISS_869_description: The ISS-869 description mentions λ_S=6,982 in the formula, but the dome's actual WIN-053 uses λ=8,619 for both poles (symmetric). Our Part 1 text correctly uses 8,619 for both. The 6,982 appears to be an error in the issue description, possibly from a draft or intermediate version. No fix needed for the scale length value itself.
